Producer Brief
Frontiers Other Topics Format Menu About
Beyond a single segment

Other
Directions

The 14 Future Frontiers are one possible series. There are other angles that could work as well — or better — depending on the audience and the slot. Eight alternative concepts, each ready to develop further.

8 series concepts
4 editorial moods
Single segment → 10-part series

Every show needs a spine — the through-line that makes individual segments add up to something. The Future Frontiers list is one spine: technology by domain. But the same technologies can be organised around what's already happening, or what choice we face, or who we become. Pick the spine; the segments follow.

Editorial moods
Investigative · Conversational · Editorial · Narrative
Sector cuts
Local · Specialist · Deep-dive · Design
The shape of the conversation we want to have
From knowledge locked in institutions, to lived evidence and measurable human outcomes.
Example T4H analysis — gap-mapping behind a public policy story
AH
Investigative

Already Happening

The future that's quietly arrived while we weren't looking.

Frontier technologies already deployed in Australia and globally — running quietly in hospitals, factories, schools, defence — that most viewers don't know exist yet. Each segment uncovers a story that was supposed to be 'years away.'

Example episodes
  • AI triage already running in NSW hospitals
  • Neural implants restoring speech in Melbourne
  • Autonomous fleets on Australian mine sites
  • Predictive infrastructure monitoring on the Harbour Bridge
Length: 8–12 min per segment Cadence: Weekly investigative slot
The emotional debt of an algorithm-shaped childhood
TH
Conversational

The Human Question

What does technology change about being human?

Less about the tech, more about the philosophical stakes. Each segment poses one human question and uses a frontier technology as the lens. Identity. Memory. Choice. Connection. Mortality. Suited to thoughtful interview formats.

Example episodes
  • Who am I if my memories live in the cloud?
  • Can a child grieve a deceased AI companion?
  • What is consent in a world that knows you better than you do?
  • Is biological age still meaningful?
Length: 10–15 min per segment Cadence: Monthly long-form
TD
Editorial

The Decision Decade

Choices we make in the next ten years that lock in the next hundred.

A series built around the idea that 2026–2036 is the load-bearing decade for AI governance, neural rights, healthcare structure, and climate-health convergence. Each segment frames one choice — and what each path leads to.

Example episodes
  • Open vs closed AI: who decides what the public can use?
  • Healthcare as platform vs healthcare as institution
  • Whose data trains the doctor of 2040?
  • Mandatory vs voluntary digital identity
Length: 10–15 min per segment Cadence: 10-part limited series
BB
Narrative

Behind the Breakthrough

Origin stories of the technologies reshaping life.

Each segment follows one breakthrough from its quiet beginning to its present moment — the researchers, the failures, the turning point. Format works for both inspiring and cautionary tales.

Example episodes
  • How mRNA went from rejected to ubiquitous
  • The 20-year arc of brain-computer interfaces
  • When AI learned to fold proteins
  • The first cellular therapy that worked
Length: 12–18 min per segment Cadence: Bi-weekly featured story
Frontier technology meets the people it's meant to serve
AF
Local

Australian Frontiers

What Australia is uniquely positioned to build, study, or solve.

A series that uses Australia's geography, demographics, mining, healthcare, defence and academic systems as the lens. Where are we ahead? Where are we behind? Where is being a small population a strategic advantage?

Example episodes
  • Why our distance is becoming a strength in healthcare
  • Critical minerals and the next compute revolution
  • Mining sites as autonomous-systems laboratories
  • Bushfire prediction as a national AI problem
Length: 8–10 min per segment Cadence: Recurring weekly slot
Old words for new realities — the language of the cognitive frontier
MM
Specialist

Mind & Machine

The cognitive frontier — where AI, BCI and human capability meet.

Deep on a narrower spine: cognition, attention, decision-making, learning, neural data. Suits a recurring expert slot rather than one-off segments. Ideal if the audience leans curious-professional.

Example episodes
  • What attention is, and what AI is doing to it
  • Cognitive ergonomics as a discipline
  • Why focus matters more than skill
  • Decision fatigue at scale
Length: 7–10 min per segment Cadence: Weekly expert slot
TQ
Sector deep-dive

Tomorrow's Doctors

How healthcare gets rebuilt around prediction, prevention and personalisation.

A healthcare-specific series. Practitioner perspectives, patient perspectives, system perspectives. Less about hype, more about what's working, what's broken, and what's coming. Strong runway across hospital, GP, allied health, and home-care contexts.

Example episodes
  • When the AI sees what the radiologist missed
  • Care at home: the hospital that isn't a building
  • Why your phone might catch your diagnosis first
  • The end of one-size-fits-all medicine
Length: 10–15 min per segment Cadence: 8-part limited series
One-size-fits-all measurement fails most humans by design
BH
Design / philosophy

Built for Humans

What it takes to design technology that actually serves people.

Where many tech shows ask 'what can this do?', this asks 'who is this for?' Covers consent, accessibility, trust, transparency, and the design choices that determine whether a technology helps or hollows out the people who use it.

Example episodes
  • Consent isn't a checkbox
  • Designing for the worst day, not the best
  • Why some AI systems lie nicely
  • Accessibility as the canary in the coal mine
Length: 8–12 min per segment Cadence: Recurring fortnightly
Mix & match

Most of these combine.
You don't have to pick one.

"Already Happening" can run weekly inside a broader Future Frontiers series. "Tomorrow's Doctors" can be a 6-episode arc inside "The Decision Decade." Spines are stackable. Happy to map combinations against any specific slot, audience and time window.

Next

Pick a spine, then pick a format. There are more options than one segment — single guest, panel, recurring slot, mini-series, deep-dive doc.

Tech 4 Humanity
An Australian AI R&D company building human-signal systems, consent infrastructure, and decision tools for the next century of work, health and identity.
